top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Granfondo Moser's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Moser's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Moser. Train it until it feels boring.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Granfondo Moser's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Granfondo Moser's mixed terrain actually does to you.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is Granfondo Moser's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
At Granfondo Moser, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Moser’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Moser. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Trento, Italy.
